01

02

A parallel ensemble of detection models scans for AI fingerprints — pixel patterns, frequency anomalies, temporal inconsistencies.

The scale of the problem

8,000,000+

deepfakes circulated online in 2025.

That's a sixteen-fold increase from 2023 — with annual growth nearing 900%. The tools to create convincing fakes are free, easy, and improving monthly. The tools to detect them have lagged behind. Until now.

See why it matters →

Why Is This AI?

We don't just tell you something is AI — we identify the specific generator that made it. Our ensemble covers Midjourney, Flux, DALL·E, and 25+ more. We analyze at pixel level, catching what single-model detectors miss. And your files are never stored or used for training.

  • Specific generator identification — not just "AI vs. human"
  • Pixel-level and frequency-domain analysis most tools miss
  • Calibrated confidence scores, not vague yes/no labels
  • Privacy first — files processed and never stored
FAQ

Common Questions,
Straight Answers.

Yes — completely free for images, video, and audio. No account, no credit card, no signup required.
Midjourney (all versions), DALL·E 2 & 3, Stable Diffusion, Adobe Firefly, ChatGPT Image, FLUX, Nano Banana, Ideogram, Grok Image, and more. We continuously update to stay current.
95%+ detection accuracy via multi-model ensemble. Results are calibrated confidence scores — not binary labels — so you always know how certain the system is.
Yes. Is This AI analyzes audio spectrograms for neural voice synthesis markers from ElevenLabs, Suno, and similar platforms. Upload MP3, WAV, or M4A.
Never. Files are processed for detection only and immediately discarded. We never store, share, or use your media for model training. See our Privacy Policy.
Images: JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, HEIC. Video: MP4, MOV, AVI, WebM. Audio: MP3, WAV, M4A, OGG, FLAC. You can also paste any public image or video URL directly into the checker.
Yes — hybrid manipulation is one of our most requested detections. Is This AI flags inpainted regions, AI-generated faces composited onto real backgrounds, and generative fill areas, along with a heatmap showing exactly which parts of the image were synthetically altered.
Our detection models are retrained continuously as new generators launch or existing ones release major updates. When a new AI tool produces content that reaches significant distribution, it enters our training pipeline. The 25+ generators we detect today represents active, rolling coverage — not a static list.
Yes. The Is This AI API lets you integrate detection directly into content moderation pipelines, CMS platforms, social networks, or any application. It returns confidence scores, generator identification, and heatmap coordinates. See API documentation for rate limits and pricing.
